title: duroia costaricensis
intro: Duroia costaricensis, also known as the "stick plant" or "stick-in-the-mud," is a species of plant in the Rubiaceae family, native to Central and South America. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be attributed to the presence of various compounds such as flavonoids and alkaloids. The health benefits of Duroia costaricensis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Duroia costaricensis is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it has been traditionally used in folk medicine in various herbal preparations, including teas and tonics.
